content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Frank R. Martinez 
President - Superintendent 
cuesta college 
P. O. Box 8106 
January 25, 1989 
San Luis Obispo, CA 
93403-8106 
Dear Mr. Martinez: 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. 1-88-473 
This is in response to your request for advice as to 
whether the Political Reform Act!! ("the Act") prohibits the 
spouse of the President of Cuesta College's Board of Trustees 
from leading an educational tour for the college. 
Since your 
reques~ concerns the duties of another person under the Act and 
was initiated by you, we treat the request as one for informal 
assistance.Y 
QUESTION 
Does the Political Reform Act ("the Act") prohibit 
Mrs. Marci Laurent, the spouse of the President of Cuesta 
College's ("the college") Board of Trustees, from leading an 
educational tour for the college? 
11 
Government Code sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory 
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise 
indicated. 
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code 
of Regulations section 18000, et seg. 
All references to 
regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code 
of Regulations. 
Y 
Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with 
the immunity provided by an op~n~on or formal written advice. 
(Government Code Section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs. Section 
18329 (c) (3) .) 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804 .. 0807 • (916)322 .. 5660 


Frank R. Martinez 
January 25, 1989 
Page 2 
CONCLUSION 
The Act does not prohibit the college from selecting Mrs. 
Laurent to lead an educational tour. 
However, Mr. Laurent is 
prohibited from participating in this decision. 
Also, section 
1090 must be considered in determining whether the college may 
select Mrs. Laurent. 
FACTS 
Cuesta college, which is part of the San Luis Obispo 
Community College District, offers educational tours through 
its community Education and Services Program. 
These tours are 
arranged through a private travel agent that is selected 
through a contract bid process. 
The groups are led by either 
college employees or community experts selected by the college. 
with the exception of the tour's leader, individuals who 
participate .in a tour pay all of their own expenses. 
Tour 
leaders do not pay expenses associated with the tour, but 
instead have their tour expenses (lodging, meals and 
transportation) paid by the travel agent. 
Tour leaders also 
are not compensated by the college for their work in leading 
the tour. 
. 
The college is considering Mrs. Marci Laurent as a leader 
for a tour to Alaska. 
Mrs. Laurent is the spouse of Bud 
Laurent, President of the college's board of trustees. 
ANALYSIS 
section 87100 sets forth the general rule concerning 
potential conflicts of interest confronting public officials. 
It states: 
No public official at any level of state or local 
government shall make, participate in making or 
in any way attempt to use his official position 
to influence a governmental decision in which he 
knows or has reason to know he has a financial 
interest. 
This rule makes clear that an official with a potential 
conflict of interest may not participate in a government 
decision affecting his or her financial interests. 
However, 
neither this rule nor any other part of the Act preclude the 
government agency involved from actually making the decision. 
Therefore, the question we consider is whether Mr. Laurent can 
participate in the college's decision of whether or not to 
select Mrs. Laurent as a tour leader. 


Frank R. Martinez 
January 25, 1989 
Page 3 
As part of the San Luis Obispo Community College District, 
the college is an entity of local government covered by section 
87100.~ Furthermore, Mr. Laurent, as a member of the 
college's board of trustees, is a public official.1I 
section 87103 defines "financial interest in a decision" 
within the meaning of section 87100. 
It states that a public 
official has the requisite financial interest if it is 
reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material 
financial effect on the official or a member of his or her 
immediate family. 
section 82029 includes the official's spouse in his 
"immediate family." 
It is also reasonably foreseeable that a 
decision by the board of trustees on whether or not to select 
Mrs. Laurent as a tour leader will have a financial effect on 
her. 
We next consider, therefore, whether the board's decision 
will have a "material" financial effect on Mrs. Laurent. 
Regulation 18702 sets forth the general approach to the 
question of whether the effect of a decision is material. 
If 
the decision affects an official's immediate family then the 
question of materiality is analyzed under Regulation 18702.1. 
Subdivision (a) of Regulation 18702.1 states that the 
effect of a decision is material if: 
(4) 
... The decision will result in the ... 
expenses ... of the official or his or her immediate 
family increasing or decreasing by at least $250. 
In applying these provisions to the facts of this matter, 
it is apparent that Mr. Laurent cannot participate in the 
decision of whether or not to select Mrs. Laurent as a tour 
leader. 
This is because Mrs. Laurent's expenses for the Alaska 
tour will be paid if she is in fact selected as tour leader. 
Since the expenses include the costs of transportation, meals 
and lodging, it is likely that they will far exceed the $250 
threshold under Regulation 18702.1. 
~ section 82041 defines "local government agency" as "a 
county, city or district of any kind .... " 
11 
section 82048 defines "public official" as "every 
member, officer, employee or consultant of a state or local 
government agency .... " 


Frank R. Martinez 
January 25, 1989 
Page 4 
Finally, please note that the facts of this matter may give 
rise to the application of Section 1090. 
The Commission has no 
jurisdiction over the application of Section 1090 and therefore 
cannot render a decision pertaining to that section. 
Please 
contact your legal counselor the Attorney General in this 
regard. 
I hope that this response has been of assistance to you. 
However, should you have additional questions please contact me 
at (916) 322-5901. 
DMG:SH: ld 
Sincerely, 
Diane M. Griffiths 
Gener 7ftl 
coun,)/ 
,'. 
~'" 
(f..-V 
/ 
( L L 
LL-. -. 
By: 
Scott Hallabrin 
Counsel, Legal Division 


CUESTA COLLEGE 
P.O. Box 8106· San I..uis Obispo. CA 93403-8106· (805) 544-2943 
SAN lUIS OBISPO COUNTY COMMUNITY COllEGE DISTRICT 
December 15, 1988 
legal Division 
California Fair Political Practices Commission 
428 J Street, Suite 800 
P. O. Box 807 
Sacramento, California 
95804-0807 
-
co 
co 
Gentlemen: 
The Community Education and Services Program of Cuesta College provides 
educational tours throughout the world where individuals may travel and 
study under the direction of an educational resource person (leader) 
appointed by the college. 
We have been conducting these tours for over 
seven years with the leaders being both college employees and selected 
community experts, who receive no compensation from the college 
district. 
(The leader is eligible to receive certain land and air 
arrangements by the travel agent--not the district.) 
This coming spring semester, we are planning a study tour to Alaska. 
We 
are considering Mrs. Marci laurent for the position as leader, who is 
the wife of the current President of our Board of Trustees, Mr. Bud 
laurent. 
We are asking her to lead this tour because of a previous 
experience where she and her husband were participants in a similar 
research expedition. 
She may be eligible to receive the cost of air transportation from here 
to Alaska and return and hotel accommodations from the travel agent, and 
nine evenings aboard an intersea research vessel. 
There would be no 
compensation in cash whatsoever other than the lodging, meals, and 
transportation she would receive as provided by the travel agency and 
research vessel. 
The individuais on the trip pay for their own 
expenses. 
A travel agency usually provides the travel arrangements for 
a tour leader for every 12 to 15 individuals who have paid their way for 
the study tour. 
College district expenses f~r such a trip are only those of the normal 
office work to provide publicity for the study tour and the exchange of 
correspondence leading up to the commitment between the travelers on the 
trip and the travel agency. 
The travel agency that conducts the trip 
receives the appointment for the trip through a bidding process. 
Once 
the trip is scheduled by the college and the travel agency is selected, 
this determines the end of much of the work done by the college; since, 
at this point, most other correspondence and action is between the indi-
vidual on the tour and the travel agency. 
.... j 


Legal Division 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
December 15, 1988 - 2 
It has been suggested that I write to the Legal Division of the Fair 
Political Practices Commission in order to receive a reply as quickly as 
possible in order to complete our plans as stipulated in this letter. 
Thank you for your attention to this matter. 
We would appreciate a 
reply as soon as possible. 
cordial~:j !? );1~ 
Frank R. Martinez 
President-Superintendent 
FRM/sc 
dw654 


California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Frank R. Martinez 
President-superintendent 
Cuestra College 
San Luis Obispo County 
Community College District 
P.O. Box 8106 
December 22, 1988 
San Luis Obispo, CA 
93403-8106 
Re: 
88-473 
Dear Mr. Martinez: 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform 
Act was received on December 21, 1988 by the Fair Political 
Practices Commission. 
If you have any questions about your 
advice request, you may contact Scott Hallabrin, an attorney in 
the Legal Division, directly at (916) 322-5901. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, 
or more information is needed, you should expect a response 
within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written 
advice. 
If more information is needed, the person assigned to 
prepare a response to your request will contact you shortly to 
advise you as to information needed. 
If your request is for 
informal assistance, we will answer it as quickly as we can. 
(See Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 
18329).) 
You also should be aware that your letter and our response 
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon 
receipt of a proper request for disclosure . 
• 
Very truly yours, 
}1l~MCt.~ 
Diane M. Gri~:JWs-((~/~;) 
General Counsel 
DMG:plh 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • (916)322,5660 


December 15, 1988 
Legal Division 
California Fair Political Practices Commission 
428 J Street, Suite 800 
P. O. Box 807 
Sacramento, California 
95804-0807

This coming spring semester, we are planning a study tour to Alaska.

are considering Mrs. Marci Laurent for the position as leader, who is

We are asking her to lead this tour because of a previous 
experience where she and her husband were participants in a similar

receives the appointment for the trip through a bidding process. 
Once 
the trip is scheduled by the college and the travel agency is selected,

vidual on the tour and the travel agency. 


Legal Division 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
December 15, 1988 - 2 
It has been suggested that I write to the Legal Division of the Fair 
Political Practices Commission in order to receive a reply as quickly as 
possible in order to complete our plans as stipulated in this letter. 
Thank you for your attention to this matter. 
We would appreciate a 
reply as soon as possible. 
Cordially yours, 
~ 
Frank R. Martinez 
President-Superintendent
